attacking_style: Not an attacker. Pure leadout specialist who delivers final turn in closing kilometers of sprint stages, typically 1-2km to go.
team_tactics: Lotto deploys DE BUYST in extended leadout role, working early then recovering to deliver final turn before sprinter launch. Demonstrates team depth by dual-phase workload.
terrain_preference: Hilly sprint stages where diesel engine separates from pure speedmen. Can sustain high pace over undulating circuits before final leadout.
vulnerability: Technical finish circuits expose crash risk. Physical sprint confrontations suggest potential positioning conflicts in tight finales.
key_patterns: Dual-phase leadout: works extensively early, then delivers final turn for EWAN (2 instances)
Excels on hilly sprint stages requiring sustained diesel power before finale (2 stages)
Crash risk on technical finish circuits compromises team sprint execution (2 incidents)
Physical confrontations suggest aggressive space-claiming in leadout positioning battles (2 confrontations)
form_trajectory: positive: most recent races show exceptional endurance delivering high-quality turns despite extended earlier work on demanding terrain
